title: draba turgida subsp. eriocarpa
intro: Draba turgida subsp. eriocarpa, also known as Draba eriocarpa, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly found in alpine and subalpine regions. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin irritations and inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. Draba eriocarpa is often used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used topically to soothe and calm irritated skin and wounds.
